title: Reservation seam mirrors holds; worktree lifecycle releases them
action: Wire the ledger into the reservation lifecycle. bee.mjs handleReservationsReserve (templates/bee.mjs:1107-1126): resolve topology via resolveRoots (state.mjs:695-752) — holder = worktree id for a linked-valid granted checkout, 'main' for ordinary; BEFORE the local reserve, findForeignHolds(mainRoot, holder, [path]); on hit return a typed refusal {ok:false, code:'FOREIGN_HOLD', ...} whose message names the holding checkout, feature, and expiry (rule-14 language, mirror guards.mjs cross-session-hold phrasing); on clear, do the local reserve and then mirrorHold the same path/ttl/session/cell. handleReservationsRelease additionally releaseHolds for the same holder/session/cell. handleReservationsSweep also sweepExpiredHolds. handleReservationsList gains a 'cross_worktree' section listing active ledger entries. Worktree lifecycle: performCleanup (templates/lib/worktree-store.mjs:729-781) calls releaseAllForHolder(mainRoot, id) best-effort AFTER removeGrant. For an UNGRANTED linked worktree (storeRoot===mainRoot) skip mirroring entirely. Tests: NEW standalone scripts/test_worktree_holds.mjs (import scripts/lib/test-fixture.mjs) covering reserve-mirrors/release-clears/foreign-refusal-shape/cleanup-releases; register it in scripts/run_verify.mjs SUITES ONLY IF exec-cs2a has released run_verify.mjs by then — otherwise coordinate: wait for that single registration line, or if still held at close-out, note it and register via a follow-up in the same commit window once released (reservation etiquette applies). Self-onboard mirrors + plugin trees; full verify green.

truths: A reserve in a granted worktree mirrors into the main ledger and a reserve in main mirrors as holder 'main'; an ungranted linked worktree does not double-mirror.
A reserve against a path ledger-held by a DIFFERENT checkout is refused with a typed FOREIGN_HOLD result naming holder + expiry, before any local reservation row is written.
release and sweep clear/prune the mirrored entries; worktree merge --cleanup releases every entry for the removed worktree id (best-effort, after removeGrant).
reservations list --json exposes active cross-worktree entries; behavior for repos with no worktrees/no ledger is byte-identical to today (missing ledger = empty).
Mirrors byte-identical; full run_verify.mjs green including the reservation race suite.
prohibitions: Do NOT change reservations.mjs core row semantics (reserve/release/TTL/pathsOverlap) — the seam lives in bee.mjs handlers and worktree-store cleanup.
Do NOT make reserve fail when the ledger is merely MISSING; missing = empty. Corrupt ledger at reserve time is a typed refusal, not a crash.

trace.outcome: Wired the cross-worktree holds ledger into the reservation seam: FOREIGN_HOLD pre-check + typed refusal before reserve, mirrorHold after a successful reserve, releaseHolds on release, sweepExpiredHolds on sweep, a cross_worktree section in reservations list, and releaseAllForHolder in worktree merge --cleanup after removeGrant. Ungranted linked worktrees never mirror (they already share main's own store).
trace.verify_output: PASS run_verify: 42 suite(s), concurrency=5, wall=30138ms — includes new scripts/test_worktree_holds.mjs (9/9 passed, 1300ms) and scripts/test_worktree_cli.mjs (117/117, updated for the async mergeFeatureWorktree signature). test_store_lock.mjs passed on the first run (no WSL2 flake this time).
